blocks:
Surah Ad-Dukhan (The Smoke), Makkan, its number of verses is fifty-nine verses

[The count is] seven according to the Kufan [reciters], and six according to the Basran [reciters] and the rest. بِسْمِ اللهِ الرَّحْمنِ الرَّحِيمِ (In the Name of Allah, the All-beneficent, the All-merciful)

(1) حم (Ḥā, Mīm.) (2) وَالْكِتابِ الْمُبِينِ (By the luminous Book!) (3) إِنَّا أَنْزَلْناهُ فِي لَيْلَةٍ مُبارَكَةٍ إِنَّا كُنَّا مُنْذِرِينَ (Indeed, We sent it down on a blessed night—indeed, We have been warning [mankind]—) (4) فِيها يُفْرَقُ كُلُّ أَمْرٍ حَكِيمٍ (in which every wise matter is apportioned,)

In al-Majma', narrated from al-Baqir and al-Sadiq (peace be upon them both): That is, We sent down the Quran, and the blessed night (الليلة المباركة) is the Night of Decree (ليلة القدر).

And al-Qummi narrated the same from them both and from al-Kazim (peace be upon them), adding: Allah, the Glorified, sent down the Quran therein to the البيت المعمور (the Frequented House) all at once, then it was sent down from the البيت المعمور upon the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله over the course of twenty years. فِيها يُفْرَقُ (in which is apportioned) means in the Night of Decree (ليلة القدر) كُلُّ أَمْرٍ حَكِيمٍ (every wise matter). That is, Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, decrees every matter concerning truth and falsehood, and what will occur in that year. And He has in it البداء (alteration of decree) and the Will (المشيئة); He advances what He wills and delays what He wills concerning lifespans (الآجال), provisions (الأرزاق), afflictions (البلايا), symptoms (الاعراض), and illnesses (الأمراض). He increases therein what He wills and decreases what He wills. The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله conveys it to the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him), and the Commander of the Faithful conveys it to the Imams (peace be upon them), until it reaches the Master of the Age (صاحب الزمان) (peace be upon him). And البداء (alteration of decree), the Will (المشيئة), advancement (التقديم), and delay (التأخير) are stipulated for him therein.

In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him), he said: Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, said concerning the Night of Decree: فِيها يُفْرَقُ كُلُّ أَمْرٍ حَكِيمٍ (in which every wise matter is apportioned). He says: Every wise matter (أمر حكيم) is sent down therein. And the decisive (المحكم) is not two things; rather, it is one thing. So, whoever judges by that in which there is no disagreement, his judgment is from the judgment of Allah, the Mighty and Majestic. And whoever judges by a matter in which there is disagreement, and he believes that he is correct, then he has judged by the judgment of the الطاغوت (tyrant/false deity). Indeed, the explanation of affairs is sent down year by year to the Guardian of the Authority (وليّ الأمر) on the Night of Decree. He is commanded therein concerning his own affairs with such and such, and concerning the affairs of the people with such and such. And indeed, besides that, the special, hidden, wondrous, and preserved knowledge (علم الله الخاصّ والمكنون العجيب المخزون) of Allah occurs for the Guardian of the Authority every day, similar to the command that is sent down on that night. Then he recited: وَلَوْ أَنَّ ما فِي الْأَرْضِ مِنْ شَجَرَةٍ أَقْلامٌ (And if all the trees on the earth were pens) the Ayah.

And narrated from him (peace be upon him), he said: O assembly of the Shi'a, argue using حم وَالْكِتابِ الْمُبِينِ إِنَّا أَنْزَلْناهُ فِي لَيْلَةٍ مُبارَكَةٍ إِنَّا كُنَّا مُنْذِرِينَ (Ḥā, Mīm. By the luminous Book! Indeed, We sent it down on a blessed night—indeed, We have been warning [mankind]—), for it belongs specifically to the Guardians of the Authority (ولاة الأمر) after the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله.

And narrated from him (peace be upon him), he said: When the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him) passed away, al-Hasan ibn Ali (peace be upon them both) stood up in the Mosque of Kufa. He praised Allah and glorified Him, and invoked blessings upon the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله. Then he said: O people, a man has passed away this night whom the former generations have not surpassed, nor will the latter generations ever reach him. Then he said: By Allah, he passed away on the night when the successor (وصيّ) of Moses (peace be upon him), Joshua son of Nun (peace be upon him), passed away, and the night when Jesus son of Mary was ascended, and the night when the Quran was sent down. Discussion regarding this topic has already passed in the Ninth Introduction (المقدّمة التاسعة) of this book, and the complete discussion concerning it will come in Surah al-Qadr, if Allah wills.

And narrated from al-Kazim (peace be upon him), that a Christian asked him about the esoteric interpretation (تفسير هذه الآية في الباطن) of this Ayah. He (peace be upon him) said: As for حم (Ḥā, Mīm), it is Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله, and it is in the Book of Hud (كتاب هود) which was sent down upon him, and its letters are incomplete. And as for الْكِتابِ الْمُبِينِ (the luminous Book), it is the Commander of the Faithful, Ali (peace be upon him). And as for the Night (اللّيلة), it is Fatimah (peace be upon her). And as for His saying فِيها يُفْرَقُ كُلُّ أَمْرٍ حَكِيمٍ (in which every wise matter is apportioned), He says: Much good comes forth from her: a wise man (رجل حكيم), and a wise man, and a wise man. The man said: Describe for me the first and the last of these men. He (peace be upon him) said: The descriptions are similar, but I will describe for you what comes forth from the lineage of the third of the people. And indeed, he is with you in the Books that were sent down upon you, provided you have not altered, distorted, or disbelieved—and you have done so long ago. (The rest of the حديث).

(٥) أَمْراً مِنْ عِنْدِنا إِنَّا كُنَّا مُرْسِلِينَ (5) an ordinance from Us. We have indeed been sending [apostles], according to the requirement of Our wisdom. إِنَّا كُنَّا مُرْسِلِينَ (We have indeed been sending [apostles]) it is Our custom to send Messengers with the Books. (٦) رَحْمَةً مِنْ رَبِّكَ إِنَّهُ هُوَ السَّمِيعُ الْعَلِيمُ (6) as a mercy from your Lord. Indeed, He is the All-hearing, the All-knowing, The word al-Rabb (الربّ) was placed in the position of the pronoun to indicate that Lordship (الربوبيّة) necessitated that, for it is the greatest type of nurturing (التربية). إِنَّهُ هُوَ السَّمِيعُ الْعَلِيمُ ** (Indeed, He is the All-hearing, the All-knowing) He hears the sayings of the servants and knows their conditions. (٧) رَبِّ السَّماواتِ وَالْأَرْضِ وَما بَيْنَهُما إِنْ كُنْتُمْ مُوقِنِينَ (7) the Lord of the heavens and the earth and whatever is between them, if you are certain. And it was recited (وقرئ) with kasra (بالجرّ). إِنْ كُنْتُمْ مُوقِنِينَ (if you are certain) you would know that the matter is as We have stated. (٨) لا إِلهَ إِلَّا هُوَ يُحْيِي وَيُمِيتُ رَبُّكُمْ وَرَبُّ آبائِكُمُ الْأَوَّلِينَ (8) There is no god except Him. He gives life and brings death, your Lord and the Lord of your forefathers. since there is no creator other than Him. يُحْيِي وَيُمِيتُ (He gives life and brings death) as you witness. رَبُّكُمْ وَرَبُّ آبائِكُمُ الْأَوَّلِينَ. (your Lord and the Lord of your forefathers.) (٩) بَلْ هُمْ فِي شَكٍّ يَلْعَبُونَ (9) Rather, they are in doubt, amusing themselves. a refutation of their being certain (موقنين). (١٠) فَارْتَقِبْ يَوْمَ تَأْتِي السَّماءُ بِدُخانٍ مُبِينٍ (10) So wait for the day when the sky brings a manifest smoke, so wait for them يَوْمَ تَأْتِي السَّماءُ بِدُخانٍ مُبِينٍ. (for the day when the sky brings a manifest smoke). (١١) يَغْشَى النَّاسَ هذا عَذابٌ أَلِيمٌ (11) covering the people. This is a painful punishment. it encompasses them. هذا عَذابٌ أَلِيمٌ (This is a painful punishment). It is narrated in a hadith concerning the signs (أشراط) of the Hour: "The first of the signs (الآيات) is the Smoke (الدخان), the descent of Jesus (peace be upon him), and a fire that emerges from the bottom of Aden Abyan (عدن أبين) driving the people toward the Gathering Place (المحشر)." It was asked: "What is the Smoke (الدخان)?" The Messenger of Allah (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم) recited this verse and said: "It will fill what is between the East and the West, remaining for forty days and nights. As for the believer, it will affect him like the state of a cold (الزّكام). As for the disbeliever, he will be like a drunkard, and it will exit from his nostrils, ears, and anus (دبره)." I say: Abyan (أبين) is with a sukūn (سكون) on the muwaḥḥadah (الموحّدة) [the letter bā’ (ب)] and a fatḥah (فتح) on the muthannāh min taḥt (المثنّاة من تحت) [the letter yā’ (ي)], [referring to] a man to whom Aden (عدن) is attributed. And in al-Jawami’, narrated from Ali (peace be upon him): "A smoke (دخان) will come from the sky before the Hour is established. It will enter the ears of the disbelievers until the head of one of them becomes like a roasted head (الرأس الحنيذ). It will affect the believer like the state of a cold (الزّكام). The entire earth will be like a house in which a fire has been kindled, with no gaps (خصاص) in it. This will extend for forty days." And al-Qummi (القمّيّ) said: "That is when they emerge from the grave during the Return (الرجعة). يَغْشَى النَّاسَ ** (covering the people) all of them, the darkness (الظلمة). Then they will say: هذا عَذابٌ أَلِيمٌ. (This is a painful punishment.)"

(١٢) **رَبَّنَا اكْشِفْ عَنَّا الْعَذابَ إِنَّا مُؤْمِنُونَ ** (12) Our Lord! Remove the punishment from us; we shall indeed be faithful. (A promise of faith if the punishment is removed from them.)

(١٣) أَنَّى لَهُمُ الذِّكْرى ** (13) How can the admonition benefit them? (From where is the admonition for them, and how can they remember/take heed in this state?) وَقَدْ جاءَهُمْ رَسُولٌ مُبِينٌ ** (13) ...while there has already come to them an evident apostle. (He made clear to them what is greater than this [state] in necessitating remembrance (الاذكار), consisting of signs (الآيات) and miracles (المعجزات).)

(١٤) ثُمَّ تَوَلَّوْا عَنْهُ وَقالُوا مُعَلَّمٌ ** (14) Then they turned away from him, and said, 'A taught one, a madman!' (It was said: meaning, a non-Arab boy belonging to some of Thaqif teaches him.) مَجْنُونٌ (Madman). Al-Qummi said: They said that when the revelation descended upon the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله, and he was overcome by a swoon (الغشي), so they said: He is a madman (مَجْنُونٌ).

(١٥) إِنَّا كاشِفُوا الْعَذابِ قَلِيلاً إِنَّكُمْ عائِدُونَ ** (We shall remove the punishment a little, [but] you will revert.) It was said: It means [they will revert] to disbelief (الكفر) immediately after the removal [of the punishment]. And al-Qummi means [they will revert] to the Resurrection (القيامة). He said: And if His Almighty's saying: يَوْمَ تَأْتِي السَّماءُ بِدُخانٍ مُبِينٍ ** (The day the sky brings a manifest smoke) was concerning the Resurrection (القيامة), He would not have said إِنَّكُمْ عائِدُونَ ** (you will revert), because after the Hereafter (الآخرة) and the Resurrection (القيامة), there is no state to which they would revert.

(١٦) يَوْمَ نَبْطِشُ الْبَطْشَةَ الْكُبْرى. (16) On the day We strike the great blow. Al-Qummi said: (It is) the Day of Resurrection (القيامة), and al-batsh (البطش) is seizing with might. إِنَّا مُنْتَقِمُونَ. (16) Indeed, We will take vengeance. (١٧) وَلَقَدْ فَتَنَّا قَبْلَهُمْ قَوْمَ فِرْعَوْنَ ** (17) And certainly We tried before them the people of Pharaoh, We tested them. وَجاءَهُمْ رَسُولٌ كَرِيمٌ. (17) and there came to them a noble apostle, (١٨) أَنْ أَدُّوا إِلَيَّ عِبادَ اللهِ ** (18) saying, ‘Hand over to me the servants of Allah. Send them forth with me, or أَدُّوا إِلَيَ ** (hand over to me) the right of Allah (حقّ الله) which is faith (الايمان) and acceptance of the call (قبول الدّعوة), O عِبادَ اللهِ. (18) servants of Allah. Al-Qummi (said): Meaning, what Allah has obligated (ما فرض الله) of prayer (الصلاة), charity (الزكاة), fasting (الصوم), pilgrimage (الحجّ), the Sunan (السنن), and the rulings (الأحكام). إِنِّي لَكُمْ رَسُولٌ أَمِينٌ ** (18) Indeed, I am a trustworthy apostle for you, not suspected (غير متّهم).

(١٩) وَأَنْ لا تَعْلُوا عَلَى اللهِ ** and do not be arrogant towards Him by belittling His revelation and His Messenger إِنِّي آتِيكُمْ بِسُلْطانٍ مُبِينٍ ** (and that you should not defy Allah. I bring you a manifest authority.) It is said: The mention of the trustworthy (الأمين) alongside the delivery (الأداء) and the authority (السلطان) alongside the defiance (العلاء) has a significance that is not hidden. (٢٠) وَإِنِّي عُذْتُ بِرَبِّي وَرَبِّكُمْ ** I sought refuge in Him and relied upon Him أَنْ تَرْجُمُونِ ** lest you harm me by striking or reviling. (I seek the protection of my Lord and your Lord from your stoning me.) (٢١) وَإِنْ لَمْ تُؤْمِنُوا لِي فَاعْتَزِلُونِ ** then be in isolation from me, neither against me nor for me. (But if you do not believe me, then leave me alone.) (٢٢) فَدَعا رَبَّهُ ** after they denied him, (saying): أَنَّ هؤُلاءِ قَوْمٌ مُجْرِمُونَ (These are a guilty lot.) It is said that this is an allusion to supplicating against them by mentioning what they had made incumbent upon themselves, and for that reason, He called it a supplication (دعاء). (Then he supplicated to his Lord: 'These are a guilty lot.') (٢٣) فَأَسْرِ بِعِبادِي لَيْلاً ** meaning, Allah revealed to him to travel by night إِنَّكُمْ مُتَّبَعُونَ ** Pharaoh and his soldiers will follow you when they learn of your departure. (So travel with My servants by night; you will be pursued.) (٢٤) وَاتْرُكِ الْبَحْرَ رَهْواً ** القمّيّ said: meaning, a side (جانباً) or take the path. And it is said: meaning, open (مفتوحاً) with a wide gap (فجوة واسعة), or still (ساكناً) in its state إِنَّهُمْ جُنْدٌ مُغْرَقُونَ. (And leave the sea parted. They are a host who will be drowned.) (٢٥) كَمْ تَرَكُوا ** They left behind much: مِنْ جَنَّاتٍ وَعُيُونٍ. (How many gardens and springs did they leave behind,) (٢٦) وَزُرُوعٍ وَمَقامٍ كَرِيمٍ ** adorned gatherings (محافل مزيّنة) and beautiful dwellings (منازل حسنة). (and crops and noble stations,) (٢٧) وَنَعْمَةٍ ** and luxury (تنعّم) كانُوا فِيها فاكِهِينَ ** enjoying luxury (متنعّمين). القمّيّ said: The luxury (النعمة) is in the bodies, فاكِهِينَ ** meaning, jesting with women (مفاكهين النّساء). (and luxury wherein they used to revel!) (٢٨) كَذلِكَ وَأَوْرَثْناها قَوْماً آخَرِينَ. (So it was! And We made another people inherit them.) (٢٩) فَما بَكَتْ عَلَيْهِمُ السَّماءُ وَالْأَرْضُ ** It is said that this is a metaphor for the lack of concern regarding their destruction and the lack of regard for their existence. (Neither the heaven nor the earth wept for them, and they were not granted any respite.) القمّيّ narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him): A man who was an enemy of Allah and His Messenger passed by him, and he recited: فَما بَكَتْ عَلَيْهِمُ السَّماءُ وَالْأَرْضُ وَما كانُوا مُنْظَرِينَ (Neither the heaven nor the earth wept for them, and they were not granted any respite). Then his son, al-Husayn (peace be upon him), passed by him, and he said: "But for this one, the heaven and the earth will surely weep." And he said: "The heaven and the earth wept only for Yahya ibn Zakariyya (peace be upon him) and for al-Husayn ibn Ali (peace be upon them both)." In المجمع, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), he said: "The heaven wept for Yahya ibn Zakariyya and for al-Husayn ibn Ali (peace be upon them) for forty mornings, and it wept for no one else except them." It was asked: "What was their weeping?" He said: "It would rise red and set red." In المناقب, narrated from him (peace be upon him), he said: "The heaven wept for al-Husayn (peace be upon him) for forty days with blood (بالدّم)." And narrated from al-Qa'im (peace be upon him): "Yahya (peace be upon him) was slaughtered just as al-Husayn (peace be upon him) was slaughtered, and the heaven and the earth wept for no one except them." وَما كانُوا مُنْظَرِينَ ** meaning, granted respite (ممهلين) until another time.

(٣٠) وَلَقَدْ نَجَّيْنا بَنِي إِسْرائِيلَ مِنَ الْعَذابِ الْمُهِينِ ** (30) Certainly We delivered the Children of Israel from the humiliating punishment, from the enslavement by Pharaoh and his killing of their sons. (٣١) مِنْ فِرْعَوْنَ إِنَّهُ كانَ عالِياً ** (31) from Pharaoh. He was indeed a tyrant, arrogant, مِنَ الْمُسْرِفِينَ ** (of the transgressors) in tyranny (العتوّ) and wickedness (الشرارة). (٣٢) وَلَقَدِ اخْتَرْناهُمْ عَلى عِلْمٍ ** (32) Certainly We chose them knowingly because they were deserving of that, عَلَى الْعالَمِينَ ** (over all the nations) over the nations (عالمي) of their time. Al-Qummi (said): Its wording (لفظه) is general (عام) but its meaning (معناه) is specific (خاصّ). (٣٣) وَآتَيْناهُمْ مِنَ الْآياتِ ** (33) and We gave them signs such as the splitting of the sea, the shading by the clouds, and the sending down of manna and quails, ما فِيهِ بَلؤُ مُبِينٌ ** (wherein there was a manifest trial)—a clear favor (نعمة جليّة) or an evident test (اختبار ظاهر). (٣٤) إِنَّ هؤُلاءِ ** (34) These ones meaning the disbelievers of Quraysh, for the story of Pharaoh was an interjection, لَيَقُولُونَ (surely say:) (٣٥) إِنْ هِيَ إِلَّا مَوْتَتُنَا الْأُولى ** (35) ‘There is nothing but our first death, The consequence (العاقبة) and the end of the matter is nothing but the death that removes the worldly life, وَما نَحْنُ بِمُنْشَرِينَ ** (and we shall not be resurrected.’)—meaning, we shall not be resurrected (بمبعوثين). (٣٦) فَأْتُوا بِآبائِنا إِنْ كُنْتُمْ صادِقِينَ ** (36) Bring back our fathers, if you are truthful.’ in your promise. (٣٧) أَهُمْ خَيْرٌ أَمْ قَوْمُ تُبَّعٍ ** (37) Are they better, or the people of Tubba‘, Tubba‘ al-Himyari, who marched with armies and occupied al-Hira, was a believer, while his people were disbelievers. For this reason, He condemned them but not him. In al-Majma‘, narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم: "Do not revile Tubba‘, for he had embraced Islam." And narrated from Imam al-Sadiq عليه السلام: Tubba‘ said to the Aws and the Khazraj: "Remain here until this Prophet emerges. As for me, if I were to reach his time, I would serve him and go forth with him." وَالَّذِينَ مِنْ قَبْلِهِمْ ** (and those before them?)—like ‘Ad and Thamud—أَهْلَكْناهُمْ إِنَّهُمْ كانُوا مُجْرِمِينَ ** (We destroyed them, for they were guilty.)—just as these ones are guilty (مجرمون).

(٣٨) وَما خَلَقْنَا السَّماواتِ وَالْأَرْضَ وَما بَيْنَهُما لاعِبِينَ ** (38) We did not create the heavens and the earth and what is between them in play. (We did not create them) being heedless (لاهين). In this is an alert (تنبيه) regarding the certainty of the Resurrection (الحشر). (٣٩) ما خَلَقْناهُما إِلَّا بِالْحَقِّ وَلكِنَّ أَكْثَرَهُمْ لا يَعْلَمُونَ ** (39) We did not create them except with the truth, but most of them do not know. Due to the scarcity of their reflection (نظرهم). (٤٠) إِنَّ يَوْمَ الْفَصْلِ ** (40) Indeed the Day of Separation (The Day of Separation) is the separation of truth (الحقّ) from falsehood (الباطل) and the righteous (المحقّ) from the wrongdoer (المبطل). مِيقاتُهُمْ ** (is) the appointed time (وقت موعدهم) for all of them (أَجْمَعِينَ). (٤١) يَوْمَ لا يُغْنِي مَوْلًى عَنْ مَوْلًى ** (41) The day when no friend will avail a friend (The day when no friend will avail a friend) meaning, whatever kind of friend (مولى) he may be, شَيْئاً ** (will avail him) anything (شيئاً) of benefit (الإغناء), and they will not be helped. (٤٢) إِلَّا مَنْ رَحِمَ اللهُ ** (42) except him whom Allah has mercy upon. (Except him whom Allah has mercy upon) by pardoning him (العفو عنه) and accepting intercession (الشفاعة) on his behalf. إِنَّهُ هُوَ الْعَزِيزُ ** He is the Almighty (العزيز); no one whom He wills to punish will be helped against Him. الرَّحِيمُ ** (He is) the All-merciful (الرَّحِيمُ) to whomever He wills to have mercy upon.

In al-Kafi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): This Ayah was recited to him, and he said: "By Allah, we are those upon whom Allah has mercy. By Allah, Allah made an exception (for us), and we shall avail them." And from him (peace be upon him): Allah, Glorified and Exalted is His mention, did not make an exception for any of the successors (أوصياء) of the Prophets nor their followers, except for the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him) and his followers (شيعته). So He said in His Book—and His saying is the truth—يَوْمَ لا يُغْنِي مَوْلًى عَنْ مَوْلًى شَيْئاً وَلا هُمْ يُنْصَرُونَ إِلَّا مَنْ رَحِمَ اللهُ ** (The day when no friend will avail a friend anything, and they will not be helped, except him whom Allah has mercy upon). He meant by that Ali (peace be upon him) and his followers (شيعته). And al-Qummi said: Whoever takes as friends (والى) those other than the friends (أولياء) of Allah, some of them will not avail others. Then He made an exception for those who took as friends (والى) the Family of Muhammad (may the blessings of Allah be upon them) and said: إِلَّا مَنْ رَحِمَ اللهُ ** (except him whom Allah has mercy upon) and the rest of the Ayah.

(٤٣) إِنَّ شَجَرَةَ الزَّقُّومِ ** (43) Indeed the tree of Zaqqūm Its meaning was mentioned in Sūrat al-Sāffāt. (٤٤) طَعامُ الْأَثِيمِ ** (44) is the food of the sinful, The one with many sins. Al-Qummi said: It was revealed concerning Abū Jahl. (٤٥) كَالْمُهْلِ ** (45) like molten copper, It was said that al-muhl (المهل) is that which is left (يُمهَل) in the Fire until it melts. Al-Qummi said: Al-muhl (المهل) is molten brass/copper, boiling فِي الْبُطُونِ ** (in the bellies) And it was recited with the yā' (الياء). (٤٦) كَغَلْيِ الْحَمِيمِ ** (46) like the boiling of scalding water. Al-Qummi said: And it is that which has been heated (حَمَى) and reached its extreme limit. (٤٧) خُذُوهُ ** (47) [The command will be:] ‘Seize him, This is on the intention of a command, and those commanded are the keepers of Hell (الزبانية). فَاعْتِلُوهُ ** and drag him So drag him. And al-ʿatl (العتل) is taking hold of the entirety of a thing and dragging it forcefully. And it was recited with the ḍammah (الضمّ). إِلى سَواءِ الْجَحِيمِ ** into the midst of hell. Its middle/center. And Al-Qummi said: Meaning, press him from every side, then descend with him إِلى سَواءِ الْجَحِيمِ. (into the midst of hell.)

(٤٨) ثُمَّ صُبُّوا فَوْقَ رَأْسِهِ مِنْ عَذابِ الْحَمِيمِ ** (48) Then pour over his head the torment of the boiling water. (It means) from a torment which is the boiling water (الحميم). (٤٩) ذُقْ إِنَّكَ أَنْتَ الْعَزِيزُ الْكَرِيمُ ** (49) Taste! Indeed you are the mighty, the noble! Meaning: and say that to him in mockery of him. In al-Qummi (it is narrated): And that is because Abu Jahl used to say, "I am the mighty (العزيز) and the noble (الكريم)," so he will be reproached with that in the Fire. And in al-Jawami' it is narrated: Indeed, Abu Jahl said to the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله: "There is none between its two mountains more mighty (أعزّ) or more noble (أكرم) than me." And it was recited as إنّك (with fathah), meaning: because you.

(50) إِنَّ هذا لَمَا كُنْتُمْ بِهِ تَمْتَرُونَ (50) This is what you used to doubt. This [refers to] this punishment (العذاب) which you used to doubt (تَشُكُّونَ) and dispute concerning it (تُمَارُونَ فِيهِ).

(51) إِنَّ الْمُتَّقِينَ فِي مَقامٍ أَمِينٍ (51) Indeed, the Godwary will be in a secure station, in a place of residence (مَوْضِعِ إِقَامَةٍ). And it was recited with fathah on the mīm (بِفَتْحِ الْمِيمِ) [as مَقَام]. [It is] secure (أَمِينٍ) because its inhabitant is safe from affliction (الآفَةِ) and removal (الِانْتِقَالِ).

(52) فِي جَنَّاتٍ وَعُيُونٍ (52) amidst gardens and springs.

(53) يَلْبَسُونَ مِنْ سُندُسٍ وَإِسْتَبْرَقٍ مُتَقابِلِينَ (53) They will wear garments of fine silk and brocade, facing one another. The sundus (السندس) is what is fine of silk (الحرير), and the istabraq (الإستبرق) is what is thick of it, facing one another (مُتَقابِلِينَ) in their gatherings so that some may enjoy the company of others.

(54) كَذلِكَ وَزَوَّجْناهُمْ بِحُورٍ عِينٍ (54) So shall it be. And We shall wed them to wide-eyed houris. The matter (الأمر) is thus. We paired them with them (قَرَنَّاهُمْ بِهِنَّ), and for that reason, [the verb] was made transitive using the bāʾ (بِالْبَاءِ). The ḥawraʾ (الحوراء) is the intensely white [woman], and the ʿaynāʾ (العيناء) is the one with large eyes.

In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him), he said: When the people of Paradise are admitted into Paradise and the people of the Fire into the Fire, the Lord of Might (رَبُّ الْعِزَّةِ) sends forth Ali (peace be upon him), and he settles them in their stations (مَنَازِلِهِمْ) in Paradise and weds them. By Allah, Ali is the one who weds the people of Paradise in Paradise, and that is not entrusted to anyone other than him, as an honor (كَرَامَةً) from Allah and a favor (فَضْلًا) with which Allah has favored Him and bestowed upon Him.

And in al-Qummi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), he said: The believer is wedded to eight hundred virgins (عَذْرَاء) and one thousand previously married women (ثَيِّب) and two wives from the wide-eyed houris (الْحُورِ الْعِينِ).

(٥٥) يَدْعُونَ فِيها بِكُلِّ فاكِهَةٍ ** (55) They will call therein for every kind of fruit, secure [from any harm]. They request and command the bringing of whatever fruits they desire; nothing of it is restricted by place or time. آمِنِينَ ** secure from harm (الضرر). (٥٦) لا يَذُوقُونَ فِيهَا الْمَوْتَ إِلَّا الْمَوْتَةَ الْأُولى ** (56) They will not taste death therein, except the first death, and He will save them from the punishment of hell, which is the one in the world (الدنيا) when one approaches Paradise (الجنّة) and witnesses it. Rather, they will live therein eternally. وَوَقاهُمْ عَذابَ الْجَحِيمِ. (٥٧) فَضْلاً مِنْ رَبِّكَ ** (57) as a grace from your Lord. That is the great success. They were given all of that as a favor (تفضّلاً) from Him. ذلِكَ هُوَ الْفَوْزُ الْعَظِيمُ ** because it is deliverance from detested things (المكاره) and attainment of desired goals (المطالب). (٥٨) فَإِنَّما يَسَّرْناهُ بِلِسانِكَ ** (58) Indeed, We have made it easy in your language so that they may take admonition. We made it easy by revealing it in your tongue (بلغتك), and this is a summary (فذلكة) for the Surah. لَعَلَّهُمْ يَتَذَكَّرُونَ ** means they understand it, and thereby take admonition for what they failed to remember. (٥٩) فَارْتَقِبْ ** (59) So wait; indeed they too are waiting. So wait for what will befall them. إِنَّهُمْ مُرْتَقِبُونَ means they are waiting for what will befall you.

( A Narrative Discussion )

In Thawab al-A'mal and al-Majma', narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): "Whoever persists in reciting Surat al-Dukhan (سورة الدّخان) in his obligatory (فرائضه) and supererogatory (نوافله) prayers, Allah will raise him up among the secure ones (الآمنين) on the Day of Resurrection, shade him beneath His Throne (عرشه), reckon him with an easy reckoning (حساباً يسيراً), and give him his book (كتابه) in his right hand."

And in al-Kafi, narrated from him (peace be upon him): He was asked, "How do I know that the Night of Decree (ليلة القدر) occurs every year?" He said: "When the month of Ramadan (شهر رمضان) arrives, recite Surat al-Dukhan (سورة الدخان) one hundred times every night. When the twenty-third night (ليلة ثلاث وعشرين) arrives, you will witness the confirmation (تصديق) of what you asked about."
